Furthermore, we reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the Management agreed to require the (a) Municipal Accountant (i) to course all regularly procured supplies and materials to the appropriate inventory accounts following the perpetual inventory method; and (ii) provide SLCs for supplies and materials by type/class; and (b) OIC-GSO (i) to provide SCs for supplies and materials by type/class; (ii) prepare and submit the  SSMI based on consolidated RIS to the Municipal Accountant strictly on a weekly basis; and (iii) conduct semestral count of inventories and submit RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
(a) Municipal Treasurer to (i) submit to the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers for the proper set-up of RPT and SET Receivables account at the beginning of each year; and (ii) intensify efforts to collect long outstanding RPT and SET Receivable and or initiate civil remedies for the collection thereof; and

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) submit to the Audit Team a narrative report on the circumstances of the missing documentation of long outstanding receivables; and (b) request the approval of the write-off of long outstanding receivables.

We recommended and the Management agreed to (a) make representation with the DBP so as to know the real status of the loan and the PMO’s action on the account’s transferred to them; and (b) request copy of the subsidiary records of the loan from DBP to be able to reconcile with the Loan Payable account balance of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the (a) Acting Municipal Treasurer to (i) refrain from releasing of checks to the payee or his duly authorized representative without issuance of Official Receipt from the latter; (ii) prepare summary list of all unreleased checks and attach the List of Unreleased Checks to the RCI and submit to the Accounting Department every reporting period for recording thereof; (iii) refrain from allowing other offices/Engineering Department to borrow paid vouchers of infrastructure projects; and (iv) strictly follow the Process Flow on Disbursement by Checks in accordance with Section 44, Chapter 3 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and Annex 47 of Local Treasury Operations Manual; (b) Municipal Accountant follow-up or request List of Unreleased Checks from the Treasury Department every reporting period for proper recording and reporting in the books of accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive endeavor to increase the implementation rate of its programs, projects and activities under 20% DF so as to catch-up with the incurred delays in schedule of implementation of each project contained in the APP/AIP and instruct the MPDC to thoroughly coordinate, monitor and evaluate implementation of the different development programs, projects and activities of the Municipality in accordance with the approved development plan.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the DRRMO to (a) revise its LDRRMP to include PPAs for the unexpended balance of LDRRMF and submit a copy of the same to the Auditor’s Office; and (b) coordinate thoroughly the implementation of identified PPAs for effective utilization of the 70% Disaster Prevention And Mitigation, Preparedness, Response, Rehabilitation And Recovery.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the:
(a) Municipal BAC (i) strictly comply with 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 for all the procurement activities of the Municipality; (ii) ensure that documentary requirements are complete as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and Philippine Bidding Documents (PBD), 5th Edition dated August, 2016; and (iii) submit all the lacking bidding documents to the Auditor’s Office for auditorial review;
(b) OIC – MENRO (i) attach the monitoring report of solid waste transport and disposal to the respective vouchers and include the volume of hauled solid waste in the Certificate of Compliance of the supplier for auditor’s verification; and (ii) provide the documentary requirements for the transport and disposal contract of solid waste as enumerated in Item 9.1.3.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; and
(c) Municipal Accountant prepare a checklist of the required documents and ensure the completeness of the same before payments are processed.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the LCE instruct the:
Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to:
re-study the viability of the construction and operation of sanitary landfill by looking for alternative funding, like joint ventures of public private partnerships, and its revenue-generating measures; and
revise the Solid Waste Management Plan (SWMP) of the Municipality, as appropriate, given the current operating realities; and
OIC – MENRO to:
re-implement an information and education campaign to underscore the segregation of solid waste at source; and
strictly implement the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
(a) Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned departments that the procurement of property and equipment under SEF should pass through the GSO for preparation and issuance of  PAR and property tags to end-users to establish property accountability; and
(b) General Services Officer instruct its personnel to (i) prepare a completely filled up PAR in the issuance of properties to end-users; and (ii) place property tags on all properties and equipment issued for easy identification thereof; and
(c) Municipal Accountant process all vouchers for payment of acquisition of office equipment only if a completely filled up PAR and photo of properly tagged equipment is attached as one of the documentary requirements.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the:
(a) HRMO to hire additional personnel to fill out the vacancies in the Engineering Department;
(b) Municipal Engineer prioritize the execution of programs and projects with deadlines funded by the National Government; and
(c) OIC-MPDO and Local Development Council (LDC) coordinate, monitor and evaluate programs and projects of the Municipality for the effective and efficient implementation of the PPAs.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive include the Head of the Gender and Development (GAD) Office as member of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (MDRRMC) for proper composition thereof.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
(a) instruct the BAC and/or the implementing departments to furnish the Auditor’s Office with copies of perfected contracts, purchase orders together with all the required supporting documents and the report of all on-going and to be implemented government PPAs in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 2013-004;
(b) hire additional personnel in the Municipal Engineering Office to help the existing workforce with the voluminous transactions of the Municipality; and
(c) require the officials and employees to attend trainings/seminars for them to enhance and update their knowledge and awareness on laws, rules and regulations.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive, Presiding Officer and Sangguniang Bayan, working together, pass an ordinance on sewerage and septage management.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations and the Management agreed to instruct the (a) HR require the JO personnel to personally open a bank account at the Municipality’s partner government bank on official time; and (b) Treasury to subsequently process the payment of JO personnel’s salary through the bank.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the Management agreed to require the HR, Accounting, and Budget study the impact of increasing the minimum daily wage of its JO personnel to align with the minimum daily wage authorized under Wage Order No. RB111-21 and recommend approval of the LCE, if possible.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive designate a DPO in accordance with the circulars and advisories of the NPC who will ensure compliance with the provisions of the DPA.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) centralize in a single channel, document and publish the process of granting donations and financial assistance to avoid confusions on the part of the constituents and to strengthen the internal control; and (b) work together with the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) to craft a resolution to update and craft a well-defined policy/guidelines in the granting of donations and financial assistance for its effective and efficient implementation.
We commended the Management for the efforts taken and recommended to continuously comply with the guidelines on the localization of the Magna Carta of Women.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Due to GSIS accounts to identify any over or under remittance and take the necessary adjustments; and (b) the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to (i) withhold premiums and remit the same to the GSIS within 10 calendar days following the month the contributions apply; and (ii) refund/collect over/under premiums deducted from employees as a result of reconciliation.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile the Due to BIR accounts to identify any over or under remittance and take the necessary adjustments and (b) the OIC-Municipal Treasurer, as result of the reconciliation, withhold taxes and/or remit/refund  the same from/to employees and BIR, respectively.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Due to Pag-IBIG account to identify any over or under remittance and take the necessary adjustments and (b) the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to withhold and remit the same from employees and Pag-IBIG, respectively.
We commended the Management for remittance of PhilHealth contributions within the prescribed period and recommended that they continuously comply with RA No. 10606.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the persons liable for the disallowances be required to settle their liability and/or appeal to the appropriate tribunal within the prescribed period as stated in COA Circular No. 2009-006 should the petition for review and appeal be dismissed by the Commission Proper and Regional Office No. III, respectively.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations and the Management agreed that the 

(a) Local Chief Executive create an appraisal committee that will revalue/appraise the value of the renovated/reconstructed market, slaughterhouse, municipal building, donated lots and other properties with no costs; 

(b) OIC-GSO 
(i) conduct actual count of all properties; and 

(ii) maintain PCs by class and/or type and reconcile the balances with the Accounting records; and 

(c) Municipal Accountant 
(i) maintain PPELCs by class and/or type to monitor and reconcile the balances with the PCs; and 
(ii) drop from the books of accounts the value of the disposed properties.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the (a) Municipal Accountant exert all efforts to reconcile the discrepancy between the RPT and SET Receivables and take the necessary adjustments thereof; (b) Municipal Assessor continue the representation with the Province of Bataan on linking the existing RPTAS with the PATAS so that the use of both systems will be fully optimized; and (c) OIC-Municipal Treasurer (i) coordinate with the Municipal Assessor to update and generate the duly certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due and collectible for the year; and (ii) submit to the Municipal Accountant the said list for the proper set-up of RPT and SET Receivables account at the beginning of each year.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the (a) BAC Chairman to purchase supplies and materials quarterly based on the consolidated APP; (b) Municipal Accountant (i) course all regularly procured supplies and materials to the appropriate inventory accounts following the perpetual inventory method; and (ii) provide SLCs for supplies and materials by type/class; and (c) OIC-GSO (i) provide SCs for supplies and materials by type/class; (ii) always prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant on a weekly basis the  SSMI based on consolidated RIS; and (iii) conduct semestral count of inventories and submit RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) verify the validity of outstanding receivable accounts and adjust the balances, if warranted; (b) take appropriate action/demand collection of the outstanding receivables before considering them for write-off; and (c) submit to the Audit Team the proof of exhaustion of all remedies to collect receivables as basis of the latter for the issuance of the final demand letter pursuant to COA Memorandum No. 2017-010.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant (a) compute/determine the impairment of Receivables and PPE in accordance with the PPSAS; (b) depreciate the Public Infrastructures over their estimated useful life; and (c) recognize the impairment and depreciation in the books of accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to confirm and coordinate with the PMO to establish the validity and authenticity of the inactive loans payable account with the DBP.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Mayor fill up the remaining mandatory positions in the MDRRM Office; (b) Municipal Accountant (i) adjust the overstatement of Trust Liability–DRRMF; (ii) record and transfer the infrastructure assets in the General Fund; and (iii) transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund at the end of the year; and (c) MDRRMO prepare and submit a certified monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day of the following month.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the (a) Municipal Mayor require the Local Development Council (LDC) to optimize the utilization of the 20% DF; (b) LDC, thru the MPDO, include and implement livelihood and entrepreneurship/local economic development projects in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP); and (c) Municipal Accountant use the special account for the 20% DF in charging expenditures intended for the 20% development projects.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor and ensure that the monthly submission of accounts, financial records and required reports to the Auditor’s Office are within the prescribed period to facilitate timely review and evaluation thereof.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant (a) reconcile the accounting with treasury’s books at least quarterly; (b) prepare and submit the BRS for SEF and TF accounts to the Auditor’s Office immediately; and (c) monitor and evaluate from time to time the assigned tasks of the personnel in-charge to avoid delay in the submission of BRS.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (i) speed up the operation of the sanitary landfill; (ii) strictly enforce the penalties for violators of RA No. 9003 and other environmental ordinances; and (iii) involve the barangay officials in the implementation of environmental ordinances in accordance with the requirements of RA No. 9003.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor, thru the MPDO Officer and GFPS TWG Chair, (a) endorse to the Sangguniang Bayan the amendment of the GAD Code to ensure that updated GAD-related laws are incorporated therein; (b) fully establish the Municipality’s GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive, planning, programming and policy formulation; and (b) strictly comply with the guidelines and procedures on the localization of the Magna Carta for Women (MCW).

We recommended that the (a) Presiding Officer of the SB formulate appropriate ordinance on the regulation of the operations of three-wheeled public conveyances along the Roman Super-highway within the locality to prevent undesirable consequences which might endanger both pedestrians and motorists; and (b) Municipal Engineer and Head of Mariveles Command Center to (i) inspect the condition of existing CCTVs and evaluate the need of repair and/or replacement thereof; and (ii) install additional units within the national highway of the Municipality to provide  protection, monitoring and record purposes and for the promotion of public safety and order and immediate rescue and assistance to the public in case of accidents.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue the effort of deducting the corresponding taxes from compensation of their officials and employees and all payments to suppliers/contractors and remitting the same within the prescribed period.
We recommended that the Municipality settle their audit disallowances within the prescribed period as stated in COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer (a) ensure that all collections are deposited daily and intact; (b) monitor regularly the recording in the cashbook and reconcile monthly with the report of the Accounting Office; and (c) immediately deposit all unaccounted collections. We further recommended that cashbooks be updated and balanced daily in consonance with Section 26 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume II.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the BRS on time and immediately prepare Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for adjustments for the reconciling items and furnish a copy thereof  to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Fast-track the computerization of the accounting system and pursue the hiring of additional personnel, both of which are contributory to the deficiencies noted.
observations.reason:
The attention of the municipal officials concerned have been called on the errors noted and directed them to take the appropriate corrective actions. They were likewise advised to take serious efforts to implement the audit recommendations.

Reiterated in Observation No. 6

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated prior year’s recommendation that Management comply strictly with COA Circular No. 97-002. Cash disbursing officers should always liquidate the cash advances for payroll fund within five days after the end of each pay period. Always grant cash advance to the Cash Disbursing Officer for the net amount of payroll for a pay period. 

We also recommended that the Accounting Office draw Journal Entry Voucher to record the Cash Advance for Intel Fund amounting to ₱5,000,000.00 for which Credit Notices are not yet issued by the COA Central Office.
observations.managementAction: Continue the efforts to inculcate compliance with the rules and make them integral habit in dealing with all cash advances continues.
observations.reason:
Inadequacy of personnel.




The similar recording of pre-2015 intelligence/ confidential fund cash advances with those of 2015 and onwards carries no significant accounting deviation or material misstatement of the financial records and is more consistent with the preferred practice of uniformity of accounting treatment.

Reiterated in Observation No. 7

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA, Section 39 of PD No. 1445, Section 23 (1) (3) of the Administrative Code of 1987 and other laws and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality shall endeavor to address the root cause of the deficiency, particularly the inadequacy of personnel to gradually attain full compliance with the laws and regulations cited.

The reconciliation of the computerized accounting system is in progress and the hiring of additional personnel is now in process.
observations.reason:
The reconciliation remains affected by the inadequacy of personnel and continuing increase in the volume of transactions.

The hiring of additional personnel is likewise limited by the lack of interested and qualified applicants.

Reiterated in Observation No. 11

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the MSWMB prioritize and implement the operation of a Sanitary Landfill especially upon approval of the Safe Closure and Rehabilitation of Disposal Facilities and other requirements of RA No. 9003.

We also recommended that Management involve closely the barangay officials in the strict implementation of the existing municipal solid waste management ordinances and environmental laws.

We further recommended that the LCE exercise strong political will to implement the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan and other environmental laws on preserving and protecting the environment.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 13

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management strengthen the (GFPS) for effective planning, budgeting, implementation and monitoring and ensure that all issues and concerns will be addressed in pursuit of women-empowerment and gender equality. 

We also recommended that the GFPS maintain and update regularly the GAD database through the data and results of the Community-Based Monitoring System (CBMS) and include sex-disaggregated data, the National Household Targeting System for Poverty Reduction (NHTS-PR) and Local Governance Performance Management System (LGPMS).
observations.managementAction: Strengthen the capability of the GFPS to be able to adequately respond to the responsibilities called for under the GAD.
observations.reason:
The Municipality’s GFPS and TWG are newly organized. They are still in the process of familiarizing with their GAD-related functions.

Reiterated in Observation No. 14

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Development Council comply with the mandates of DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2011-1 and Annex A of IRR of RA No. 9184 for the efficient planning, implementation and monitoring of PPAs funded under the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: The planning and monitoring of the projects shall be properly documented to provide a basis for evaluating and quantifying the impact of the development projects to the local economy.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Development Council and the Office of the Municipal Engineer have been reminded of their respective responsibilities in this regard.

Reiterated in Observation No. 10

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the cash shortage as receivable to separate the accountability of the new OIC-Municipal Treasurer in consonance with Section 54 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs. We also recommended that the OIC-Municipal Treasurer use separate cashbook pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.

We further recommended that management institute appropriate actions to recover loss funds.
observations.managementAction:
A JEV was drawn to record the cash shortage.

Directed all accountable officers to use separate cashbooks for each accountability.

Demand was made against the Collecting Officer and the Municipal Treasurer on March 24, 2014 but no restitution or deposit is so far made.

Criminal and administrative complaints for violation of Art. 217, RPC, Sec. 3(e) of RA 3019, Gross Neglect of Duty, Grave Misconduct, and Serious Dishonesty were filed in April 2014 against Ms. Ramos and Mr. Diuco at the Office of the Ombudsman.

A parallel investigation was also requested by the Municipal Mayor to the National Bureau of Investigation.
observations.reason: No restitution or deposit is made as of CY 2016.

observations.recommendation:

We also recommended that the BAC faithfully adhere to Section 17 of Article VI of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Instructed the Municipal Health Officer to ensure compliance with Administrative Order No. 163 and EO No. 49.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001 to ensure that the titles of the lots are transferred to the government before payment and that all the required documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers.

Asked the land owners to surrender their titles after payment of selling price.

Titling of lands are already in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality continue to provide at least 1% of its total budget for senior citizens and PWDs PPAs as well as operation and maintenance of an OSCA and PDAO; initiate and improve more beneficial, productive, meaningful and lasting PPAs that will lead to the promotion of their welfare in order for them to be productive citizens and contributors in nation building.
observations.managementAction: The municipality will conduct needs assessment of senior citizens and PWDs and review the existing PPAs being implemented to identify the priority PPAs that should be implemented to better respond to the needs of the senior citizens and PWDs.
observations.reason: More beneficial, productive, meaningful and lasting PPAs were not yet initiated.
